rag_rules: Preserve the existing AADL structure and attach any generated annex only to the correct owner scope without changing unrelated architecture.
Keep type-scope contracts on the component type and implementation-only content on the implementation; do not move content across scopes arbitrarily.
generic_rules: Maintain owner/scope consistency between the generated contract content and the AADL element that receives it.
Preserve existing features, subcomponents, connections, properties, and non-target annex content unchanged.
If implementation content depends on subcomponents or connections, keep it in implementation scope rather than lifting it to type scope.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE content is enclosed in an `annex agree {** ... **};` block.
Type-scope references should use only symbols visible in that owner scope; avoid subcomponent-path references in type-owned content.
anti_patterns: Do not insert a contract into an implementation when its statements belong to component type scope.
Do not rewrite architecture or introduce scope-breaking references just to accommodate generated content.
irrelevant_topics: Requirement semantics about specific input conditions
